Well, unless I haven't had a chance to meet you yet, I'm Lori Haller and I own my own graphic design studio in Washington, D.C. In my studio, it's full service and what that means is I don't just do this direct mail, I do package design, logos, mastheads, a lot of websites now, emails and you know, anything that really comes up. So what happens, just so you understand if you're perhaps just getting into copywriting or want to get into this line of work, is that sometimes clients come to me and this is what Clayton was just saying, they will come to me now and say, you know, Lori, we have this new launch, it's this new supplement with like a pomegranate in it and we really want to have you design it and who have you been working with lately, wink, wink, that you know, maybe has some controls with you and you work together as a unified team. So that happens a lot to me now and then if I already had worked with you or knew you in the past, I would go ahead and tell them like they asked for three or four people. So that can happen. The opposite happens where it's like if you are the copywriter, then perhaps you've heard of me or saw some of my winners in the past and that way it works vice versa. So what Clayton had to say is very important that we work hand in hand together. So what can happen is we'll all get together and talk about like what the product is, what it's going to do for the person and all the specifications. The copywriter will go away obviously and write the copy but I on the other hand, and this is something that you might not know yet about graphic designers, you go in and do all of your research, you know, the unique benefits, the specificities, what is it going to cost, who's going to look at it, I do the same thing. Like in a huge annoying way because most of my clients are exhausted when I get done with them in my big list. They tell me this all the time and you can hear them sigh on the phone after they see my email but I do that as well because it's very, very important for me, the designer who's going to paint this picture visually with your wonderful copy to really try to understand your copy, understand who it is that's going to read it and what do they need to see visually with your brilliant copy that you spent maybe three months on writing. What am I going to be able to tell them or show them visually that will, like it's almost like a marriage. So I always try to tell people first off when I meet them and they're a copywriter that to me in my heart, deep in my heart and soul, copy is king. It's so important and important to me and everyone else in this room but on the other side of the coin design is the queen that matches up with that and they go through the kingdom and make it like magical together. I really, really, I think probably the design really does help. Obviously I am very serious about that. So just quickly Clayton, I was just going to show this particular piece that I brought with me and I'm very excited about a week before, like about a week ago, I just found out that my piece won the control which means, and you know, tell me if I'm saying this in the wrong way for copywriters but in the past there was a particular product for women my age. What does that mean? It's a face cream, a particular moisturizer, a specific one that's more like a gel that stays on the top of the skin but then it goes in, you know, there's all these really peculiar things about this face cream so they've been advertising it with a direct mail campaign like such as this and so these three campaigns here and you can look at them like through the course of the seminar but these three mailed like, I think this was like three years ago and then this design and copy was a couple years ago and then this one was recently and it won like the most so it's the new control. So the copywriter Kim Krause, Schwam and I go in, she writes a copy, we do this long, you know, series of meetings, I exhaust the client, drive them crazy with all my questions and so we come up with this piece. So our piece just mailed and we just got the information back and like I said, this control is now going to be rolled out which means, you know, maybe millions, this is for healthy directions, I don't know what their core list quantity is but it'll be rolled out in June and hopefully again and again and again Kim and I will be called back to update this or create a new package together. So I'm hoping that helps solve the mystery because those are the questions that came up last night and this morning at lunch, like where do I fit into the equation as graphic designer so that's how you got into this and how you found your first client. Alright well I'll tell you really quickly like how I first discovered that I was going to do this or not be a race car driver or you know some of those other things. I don't know that was on the list but you know when I was seven or eight and I was fooling around with you know you're starting to write letters and numbers and things like that my grandma asked me to do these thank you notes and she gave me all these little things and you know I knew how to write and letters and stuff and so she's like can you just make these thank you notes for me and so probably everyone else in the room is like what the heck is she you know what direction is she going with this. So probably for everyone else in here you know you just wrote out the letters you drew a flower on it I don't know. When I started drawing these letters just to me it's so personal like the A sender on the H and then the A is so round and then you know all that stuff I'm like as a little kid you know really entranced by it it's like so it became my passion my true passion so you know I went on to art school and tried to get some jobs full time like some of the other people in the room I had to learn I mean I had to learn from the best so I went from agency to agency you know trying to find the best typographer the best person with models the best with film I've done a lot of film radio TV and you know different directions so I did that but then I was like I really want to have my own company so I don't know maybe it was like 15 years ago I started really just deciding I'm not going to work and you guys might be in that position too. You know you're working full time you have this passion on the side and you're like I want to jump over and Carleen you probably I think we worked together when I was full time and I had told you that as well like I really want to just go out of my own I want to have my own agency and try to accomplish my goals so I did I just told Walter Pierce who was so lovely with me at KCI and that's where I started working with Clayton's copy that I just I want to give it a try on my own and he was so behind me and so helpful and perhaps this would be a good tip for you as well you know maybe you're working somewhere full time you're doing copy or whatnot and they will give you some clients as well that's what I did and 15 years later you know I'm still you know I've got three kids they're still fed pretty much you know most of the time no but I've been very very blessed and lucky to make that happen but one of the things I wanted to tell you about is a story that's going to make me cry about Carleen but no no so one idea or a little tip I want to give you and this is sort of similar to what some of the other people have said but it's a little different there are I'd say around eight of us women in this one area before Carleen moved away it was myself a designer Beth Sokoloff from Phillips also a mom Carleen and your yeah and Kim and two or three other women and we decided that all of us had just jumped or had decided to jump soon so like I still had a little one and some people were pregnant and we all decided to come once a month at least and sit and talk with each other at the Starbucks you know get a babysitter or whatever so I had worked with Carleen at Georgetown Publishing on stuff and she had seen my work and we got to know each other but she didn't know me know me you know I have a unique way of working my agency is very different I go in kind of like as a businessman first and then I'm the art girl on the side you know I want to increase the bottom line I have all these goals she didn't know that about me so during these it was about a year and then you had to move away well I know that was pretty bad so Carleen moved away so that was kind of my chance to allow her to get to know me I like you know we it was give and take with each person in the group Beth I ended up hiring her for a lot of projects Kim and I started working on projects the other woman Lindsay mains you know we would help each other guys I'm stuck at home all day I can't write my copy somebody would say until late at night the other woman and this doesn't just have to be women obviously twist the story around it can be men you don't have to meet in person that's just what girls do kind of but so what happened was Carleen ends up getting a call a couple years later from Oprah and they had asked her to write the copy and so Carleen needed an art director and she called me you know there's a million art directors out there and our joke is that Rob Davis the king of art direction wasn't you know available that day so she was stuck no one else was available so she had to call Lori but seriously that wouldn't have happened to me if we wouldn't have done that she would have only known this much of me and my potential and what I stood for I was able to whether we did it on the phone or maybe a Skype now you know this is like 10 years or five years you know we didn't have Skype then I was able to let her you know come inside and see what my company was like see what my personality was like so that is a way that perhaps you guys could work together is getting to know each other and then say you get a job and then you need another copywriter this just really worked well for me and now really everybody will go on my website that was just one project it wasn't any different than this I did the same thing it just happened to be Oprah so people are like you know I still do $25 business cards for the drive cleaner down the street but they're like you did Oprah you know like oh yeah that was me so that probably well not just probably that catapulted my career hugely and I really thank Carleen for that and I was able to hug oh yes she's being very modest because when I got the assignment and they told me they said hey we like you to you know let us know a couple or two or three designers that you like to work with and I said I only have one I said it's Lori Hallard that's who I want to work with and there she's amazing because they want us to do something different and I'm just going to be creative and this you're just seeing just a little bit of what Lori is I mean you're talking about the you know just a large amount of enthusiasm and just passion for what she does and I thought wow this is one of the biggest opportunities I have I want somebody who has who's going to be equally excited about this and crazy passionate about it and so she says you know Rob Davis Rob wasn't even in the running although I love Rob I love Rob Davis I love him but he wasn't it was always Lori and so when I called him when I called her what was it okay so I'm like this is what I hear and then it was like Oprah and I was like I don't know who it is on the phone but it sounds good to me yeah I know oh we're going to get it Lori say Lori she's going to be a great designer and she's going to be a great designer and she's going to be a great designer Lori say Lori shared that story with me last night and I hadn't heard her perspective from that you know and it was very you know touching because we do have a friendship a work relationship and it's so nice when you're successful that's wonderful but it's also amazing to help others be successful you get more joy really out of that you look around you see people that hey we struggled together and then when we're making it big together and we're helping one another and so you can always find a way to help somebody no matter what your situation is and so it always pans out and always works out so just find ways to do it definitely no thank you Carlin thank you and you know one last thing the other woman there that I mentioned Kim Crousey Schwam who wrote this piece so about three weeks ago and I for this whole year my dream you know how you wake up and you want a whole you know a dream my dream for this whole year and moving in the next five years I really have my heart set on getting clients in Paris London and New York that's just I don't know how that came to me but I'm like I am hell bent and I am going to get that you know so I like doing this thing where well you probably don't want to do this but you could try it at home if you want to do it because like put it out to the universe you know tell everybody like you know I tell people now I'm trying to get clients in London, Paris and New York you know do you know anybody that needs art or whatever so Kim calls me and she says gosh I'm doing this big huge project and I already knew it because when I kept going over to her house to work on this we lived near each other and she kept giving me all these smoothies to try and I and they were delicious so she was working on this huge client in London the Green Gate London beautiful website logo package designed by a London agency I was feeling really like oh you know so I was looking at all of her stuff and then she calls me and she says I really hate to ask this of you but the fancy London agency is too busy to put the copy into the layout so they were so not snobby but you know they were like they don't want to put the copy in the layout and see how long the copy is or isn't or figure it out they just wanted to do the fancy design and at first you know my feelings were a little like I wish I was a person I'd love to have a London A- you know that's my dream and I like tried to pick myself up on my bootstraps and I'm like come on just okay so I said Kim for free we've been friends since these coffee things for crying out loud I will float it was like two or three documents and also it was helping Kim I you know I don't know where you are in your copy career but she's like I don't know if it's too long too short I don't know it's an odd client and it's supposed to be really sleek and these beautiful photographs and stuff like that I'm like please let me do it I figured out millimeters yeah and I went ahead and gave them to her for free and no big deal you know it's two or three hours worth of work I adore Kim and so then she sent it to the client that cut the copy she was like so apologetic she had to do it two or three times then it was going to the fancy London you know place and lo and behold the next day I get an email and this woman Inessa that owns the company she emailed me and she had heard what I had done and she wanted to use me you know so yeah I was like hot diggity dog so I just finished that up like I'm not lying three hours before I got on the plane what I ended up doing she had absolutely no budget that's probably what happened I sucked it up I'm like I don't care you know I want this client bad so probably for one of the projects I probably got you know that chunk of money probably paid for that there were two other so I pretty much worked my you know what off for a good couple weeks I also provided another idea because these were brochures and whatnot banners that were going to this huge trade show for these fancy health products I'm like Lori there's 5000 different attendees and vendors and expose and people that are going there you just knock yourself out on these all those people are maybe going to ask Inessa because I did a beautiful job I know I did who did your art so I gave her another free thing I even wrote the copy on it and I told her how about if we do two times a day these free raffle giveaways and I just make these little raffles on a piece of 8.5 by 11 piece you know paper you chop it you give it away you try to bring the people in there they want all these free products and stuff and so I put that together too and she just emailed last night that everything is back from the printer the show happens Sunday and Monday and she has a lot of other things now she wants to talk to me about so I will leave you with that I wish you the best of luck and I just find like probably the best thing is giving back you know giving that part of yourself back perhaps and using your craft and what you're really good at in life has really paid off for me and good luck to you thanks for your time you
